calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-29-2023 Off Calendar

Defendant Nationwide Life And Annuity Insurance Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion To Sever Plaintiffs Claim For Writ Of Mandate Against The Commissioner Of The California Department Of Insurance
Off Calendar Aug-24-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Kelly Graf, Esq. 213-623-9300 Dentons US LLP for Defendant Nationwide Life And Annuity Insurance Companys.
Jak Nick, Esq. Deputy Attorney General, 213-269-6173 for Defendant Ricardo Lara.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. the Court announces it did not receive a contest email. Ms. Graf informs the Court she is not contesting the tentative ruling. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

1 - DEFENDANT NATIONWIDE LIFE AND ANNUITY INSURANCE COMAPNY'S Motion To Sever Plaintiffs Claim For Writ Of Mandate Against The Commissioner Of The California Department Of Insurance.

The "motion to sever plaintiff's claim for writ of mandate against the Commissioner of the California Department of Insurance" is granted.

Joining a writ action against the insurance commissioner to a bad faith case against an insurer is a recurring tactic by this plaintiffs' counsel. (Memo. 3:3-5:15.) The tactic has been rejected by severance four times in recent years by three judges of this court. (Id. at 5:16-6:12; RJN A-E.) The same severance issues are present in this case, so the court incorporates the prior four orders by reference, grants judicial notice and adopts the prior orders' findings.

Plaintiff is free to litigate the writ claim.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)

Joinder Of Defendant Ricardo Lara As Commissioner Of The Ca Doi To Nationwide Motion To Sever
Off Calendar Aug-24-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Kelly Graf, Esq. 213-623-9300 Dentons US LLP for Defendant Nationwide Life And Annuity Insurance Companys.
Jak Nick, Esq. Deputy Attorney General, 213-269-6173 for Defendant Ricardo Lara.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. the Court announces it did not receive a contest email. Ms. Graf informs the Court she is not contesting the tentative ruling. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

2 - DEFENDANT RICARDO LARA'S Joinder Of Defendant Ricardo Lara As Commissioner Of The Ca Doi To Nationwide Motion To Sever.

The "motion to sever plaintiff's claim for writ of mandate against the Commissioner of the California Department of Insurance" is granted.

Joining a writ action against the insurance commissioner to a bad faith case against an insurer is a recurring tactic by this plaintiffs' counsel. (Memo. 3:3-5:15.) The tactic has been rejected by severance four times in recent years by three judges of this court. (Id. at 5:16-6:12; RJN A-E.) The same severance issues are present in this case, so the court incorporates the prior four orders by reference, grants judicial notice and adopts the prior orders' findings.

Plaintiff is free to litigate the writ claim.

Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-11-2023 Continued To Oct-11-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
